lines.line: >Stephanie Milne-Lane begins the oral history interview and Reverend Ruth Marsh explains how she moved to Idaho to take the pastor position at Trinity United Methodist Church in Idaho Falls.

metadata.title: Introductions
metadata.synopsis: Stephanie Milne-Lane begins the oral history interview and Reverend Ruth Marsh explains how she moved to Idaho to take the pastor position at Trinity United Methodist Church in Idaho Falls.
metadata.keywords: Idaho Falls, Idaho, Trinity United Methodist Church

lines.line: >Marsh explains how she first heard about the COVID-19 pandemic through NPR. She shares what life has been like for her since the pandemic started in 2020. Marsh also explains how her church adapted due to the stay at home order and the opening of a day shelter at Trinity United Methodist Church.

metadata.title: COVID-19
metadata.synopsis: Marsh explains how she first heard about the COVID-19 pandemic through NPR. She shares what life has been like for her since the pandemic started in 2020. Marsh also explains how her church adapted due to the stay at home order and the opening of a day shelter at Trinity United Methodist Church.
metadata.notes: Day Shelter Omicron Variant Worship Facebook

lines.line: >Marsh explains how different church service is since the Covid-19 pandemic began. She also articulates how she and her staff have made decisions regarding on gatherings, masking, and vaccinations throughout the pandemic.

metadata.title: Church Service
metadata.synopsis: Marsh explains how different church service is since the Covid-19 pandemic began. She also articulates how she and her staff have made decisions regarding on gatherings, masking, and vaccinations throughout the pandemic.

lines.line: >Marsh provides insight into the opening of the day shelter at Trinity United Methodist Church during the pandemic. She describes how opening the church up to the less fortunate caused tension in the congregation.

metadata.title: Day Shelter
metadata.synopsis: Marsh provides insight into the opening of the day shelter at Trinity United Methodist Church during the pandemic. She describes how opening the church up to the less fortunate caused tension in the congregation.
metadata.keywords: Day Shelter, Renovations, United Methodist Women, Scouts

lines.line: >Marsh articulates the efforts she and her staff made to make information on vaccinations available to the congregation. Marsh and Milne-Lane discuss the growing need of people in the Idaho Falls community that Marsh serves through the day shelter. Marsh also explains the toll of her work has had on her during the pandemic.

metadata.title: Vaccinations

Marsh and Milne-Lane discuss the growing need of people in the Idaho Falls community that Marsh serves through the day shelter. Marsh also explains the toll of her work has had on her during the pandemic.

lines.line: > Marsh notes how Trinity United Methodist Church was able to secure Covid relief grant money.

metadata.title: Grant Funding

lines.line: >Marsh articulates what success has been like for her professionally and personally during the Covid-19 pandemic. She notes her church operated on budget and noted the tremendous giving spirit of her congregation. She also shares the heartache she has experienced.

metadata.title: Success
metadata.synopsis: Marsh articulates what success has been like for her professionally and personally during the Covid-19 pandemic. She notes her church operated on budget and noted the tremendous giving spirit of her congregation. She also shares the heartache she has experienced.

lines.line: Marsh shares her observations on rights and responsibilities when it comes to the pandemic. She closes the interview by underscoring that people have the capacity to build community and support one another, even during a pandemic.

metadata.title: Conclusion
metadata.notes: Marsh shares her observations on rights and responsibilities when it comes to the pandemic. She closes the interview by underscoring that people have the capacity to build community and support one another, even during a pandemic.

children.text: >Stephanie Milne-Lane begins the oral history interview and Reverend Ruth Marsh explains how she moved to Idaho to take the pastor position at Trinity United Methodist Church in Idaho Falls.

>Marsh explains how she first heard about the COVID-19 pandemic through NPR. She shares what life has been like for her since the pandemic started in 2020. Marsh also explains how her church adapted due to the stay at home order and the opening of a day shelter at Trinity United Methodist Church.

>Marsh explains how different church service is since the Covid-19 pandemic began. She also articulates how she and her staff have made decisions regarding on gatherings, masking, and vaccinations throughout the pandemic.

>Marsh provides insight into the opening of the day shelter at Trinity United Methodist Church during the pandemic. She describes how opening the church up to the less fortunate caused tension in the congregation.

>Marsh articulates the efforts she and her staff made to make information on vaccinations available to the congregation. Marsh and Milne-Lane discuss the growing need of people in the Idaho Falls community that Marsh serves through the day shelter. Marsh also explains the toll of her work has had on her during the pandemic.
> Marsh notes how Trinity United Methodist Church was able to secure Covid relief grant money.

>Marsh articulates what success has been like for her professionally and personally during the Covid-19 pandemic. She notes her church operated on budget and noted the tremendous giving spirit of her congregation. She also shares the heartache she has experienced.

Marsh shares her observations on rights and responsibilities when it comes to the pandemic. She closes the interview by underscoring that people have the capacity to build community and support one another, even during a pandemic.

indexes.title: Index to Reverend Ruth Marsh Oral History Interview

story.description: Rev. Ruth Marsh shares her experiences as the pastor of Trinity United Methodist Church.
